statement: Run partial-failure progression is currently broken by design and should be fixed before the next pilot loop by introducing explicit downstream skip/fail propagation and allowing runs to complete with failures instead of halting on any failed target.
rationale: The current progress model leaves downstream work permanently pending after upstream failures and treats any stage failure as terminal even though reporting code already models completed-with-failures semantics.
keyAssumptions: The intended product behavior is to continue with successful units when some stage units fully fail.
validationCriteria: A run with one failed rubric and many successful rubrics still advances successful units to later stages.
Pending counts resolve cleanly via explicit skip/fail marking.
Run summaries end in completed + has_failures when appropriate.
critiquePoints: The engine may intentionally prefer strict all-or-nothing semantics for rubric stages.
questions: Should downstream dependents be marked skipped or failed when prerequisites are missing?
